problem: Options-MC currently labels artifacts as `data_quality_status=OK` even when realized-vol inputs are stale by ~12+ days (`calibration.rv_freshness_seconds` ~1,088,000s from local fallback). This can produce false confidence in edge attribution and gate decisions because IV-vs-RV comparisons are treated as valid despite stale RV context.

evidence.symptom: `edge_attribution.iv_rich_vs_rv` and downstream quality/gate interpretation proceed under stale RV without explicit fail code.

required_change: Introduce an explicit RV freshness SLA for options-MC (configurable, default <= 3600s) and evaluate it before scoring/gating.

Fail closed when RV freshness SLA is breached: set `data_quality_status=DATA_QUALITY_FAIL: stale_realized_vol`, set `edge_attribution.explainable=false` with reason `stale_realized_vol`, and force `gates.allow_trade=false`.
Emit machine-readable RV freshness metadata and SLA decision fields (`rv_freshness_sla_seconds`, `rv_freshness_pass`, `rv_staleness_reason`) in every artifact.
Keep deterministic fallback behavior, but do not treat fallback RV as valid when stale beyond SLA.
Add telemetry counter `options_mc_runs_rv_stale_events` for monitoring feed quality regressions over time.
acceptance_tests: Unit test: fresh RV (primary or fallback) within SLA -> `data_quality_status` remains non-fail and `rv_freshness_pass=true`.
Unit test: RV freshness exceeds SLA -> artifact returns `DATA_QUALITY_FAIL: stale_realized_vol`, `edge_attribution.explainable=false` with reason code, and `gates.allow_trade=false`.
Unit test: missing RV remains `DATA_QUALITY_FAIL: missing_realized_vol` (no regression of existing fail code).
Integration test: replay stale fixture from latest experiments -> zero artifacts with stale RV report `data_quality_status=OK`.
Regression test: ranking/notification path excludes `DATA_QUALITY_FAIL: stale_realized_vol` artifacts from trade-ready candidate generation while preserving explicit operator diagnostics.
constraints: Do not relax existing risk/tail/stress gates.
Preserve backward compatibility with additive fields and reason codes.
Prefer fail-closed behavior over implicit stale-data acceptance.
